+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current keycloak setup",
+        "description": "Verify your keycloak version and configuration:\n```bash\nkc.sh --version 2>/dev/null || bin/kc.sh --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working keycloak installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review keycloak configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant keycloak configuration:\nThere is currently a get user by id and a get users with query string and search filtering. Can we get an id field added to the search for users API that takes in an array of user ids?"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Get multiple users by Ids",
+        "description": "My use case was a \"Member\" relation similar to private GitHub Repositories where you can invite Users as \"Members\" (or other roles, a.k.a. Collaborators).\n\nIn my case a Member is a relationship object for a many-to-many relation between (something like) Repositories and Users.\n\n```ts\ninterface Member {\n  projectId: string; // whatever\n  userId: string; // keycloak uuid\n}\n```\n\nMy backend would provide a list of members given a project id:\n\n```http\nGET\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Get multiple users by Ids\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],

+      "summary": "My use case was a \"Member\" relation similar to private GitHub Repositories where you can invite Users as \"Members\" (or other roles, a.k.a.
